reasoning: I came to the booth still carrying what I learned in those months after 2020 — that anger-voting only redistributes failure, it does not repair it. Five years on, I have watched the Center's indifference to Bengal match what I once condemned in Mamata. The truckshed is still there. My students' fathers are still cycling between the cities and home with nothing in their pockets. Shantanu Thakur sits as a minister, yes, but his Matua constituents remain where they were. I am no longer young enough to believe that swapping one failure for another buys progress. NOTA is not hope — it is honesty.
primary_drivers: Realization that his 2019 anti-TMC vote failed to deliver promised competence or change
Intellectual integrity — refusal to vote against something out of anger again
Disillusionment with both BJP's 'absent indifference' and TMC's entrenched corruption
